title: PersistentVolumeClaims: Giving pods durable storage
scenario: The `postgres` pod is `Pending` because it tries to mount a PersistentVolumeClaim named `db-pvc` that doesn't exist yet. Create a StorageClass, a PersistentVolumeClaim (10Gi, ReadWriteOnce), and watch the PVC bind automatically via dynamic provisioning.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: standard
provisioner: kubernetes.io/no-provisioner
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
reclaimPolicy: Delete
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: db-storage
  namespace: default
spec:
  storageClassName: standard
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteMany
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 5Gi

objectives.description: PVC requests at least 1Gi of storage

hints.text: Apply the StorageClass first — the PVC can't reference it until it exists. You can apply both in one multi-doc YAML.
The PVC `storageClassName` must exactly match the StorageClass `metadata.name`: `fast-ssd`.
Once the PVC is Bound, the pod will be scheduled and transition to Running. Check the Cluster View to see PVC → pod relationships.

solution:
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: fast-ssd
provisioner: kubernetes.io/no-provisioner
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
reclaimPolicy: Delete
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: db-pvc
  namespace: default
spec:
  storageClassName: fast-ssd
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Gi
